Issue: i can not able to see apply tab
Subject: I need to practice, submissions not working
response: To practice and resolve the issue with submissions not working, ensure you are following the correct steps for the type of event you are participating in. For individual events, a single 'Candidates' tab is displayed, while for team-based events, both 'Teams' and 'Candidates' tabs are available. If you are unable to see the 'Apply' tab, check the event type and the layout of the 'Candidates' section. If issues persist, refer to the comprehensive HackerRank Support Documentation for further assistance.
justification: The provided context does not directly address the issue of not being able to see the 'Apply' tab, but it offers guidance on navigating the HackerRank platform for different types of events and suggests consulting support documentation for unresolved issues.

Issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
Subject: Certificate name update
response: You can update the name on your certificate only once per account. To update the name on your certificate: 1. Open your certificate page. 2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display. 3. Click Regenerate Certificate. 4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.
justification: Context 1 provides the steps to update the name on a certificate.

Issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
Subject: Website Data crawl
response: To stop Claude from crawling your website, you can modify the robots.txt file in your top-level directory by adding the following lines: User-agent: ClaudeBot Disallow: /. This will block Claude's crawler from accessing your entire website. Please do this for every subdomain that you wish to opt out from.
justification: Context 1 provides information on how to block the Claude crawler by modifying the robots.txt file.

Issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
Subject: Claude for students
response: To set up a Claude LTI key for your students, follow these steps: 1. In Canvas, sign in as an administrator and navigate to Admin -> Developer Keys. 2. Click '+ Developer Key' then '+ LTI Key.' 3. Enter the required information, including Key Name, Description, Redirect URIs, Title, Target Link URI, OpenID Connect Initiation Url, and JWK method. 4. Under Additional Settings, toggle Privacy Level to 'Public.' 5. Under Placements, remove the defaults and add 'Course Navigation' and 'Assignment Edit' as the options. 6. Click 'Save.' 7. Toggle the state to 'On.' For more detailed instructions, refer to the 'Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure' article.
justification: The provided context includes a step-by-step guide on how to set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure, which is relevant to the user's question.
